feed.title: I build World Models. Tumors break them.
feed.subtitle: We build generalizable, uncertainty-aware world models for tumor evolution and metastasis, models that (i) learn causal/functional links from genotype to regulatory state to phenotype, (ii) explicitly represent measurement noise and batch effects in real sequencing data, and (iii) transfer across cancers, tissues, and platforms. The scientific payoff is how somatic/germline variation rewires regulatory programs, how early genetic events bias invasive and metastatic routes, and how chromosomal instability (CIN) and tumor-intrinsic programs enable organ-specific colonization. The translational payoff is cheaper, scalable profiling and cleaner data, enabling robust biomarkers and therapeutic hypotheses.

entry.title: Welcome to my research blog
entry.content:
Welcome to my research blog.

I use this space to share brief updates on my work in cancer genomics, single-cell and spatial biology, and AI-based world models for tumor evolution and metastasis.

My research focuses on how genomic alterations reshape regulatory programs, how early events bias metastatic trajectories, and how uncertainty-aware computational models can improve biological discovery and translation.

Here I will post:

  • research updates,
  • method development notes,
  • publication highlights,
  • grant and project ideas,
  • and perspectives on computational oncology.

Thank you for visiting.
entry.summary: Research notes on cancer genomics, single-cell biology, and AI-driven modeling.

entry.title: a post with giscus comments
entry.content: This post shows how to add GISCUS comments.
